skill: Linear equations — solving for a variable
source: Derived from error patterns across CB SAT Practice Tests (data/cb-corpus/)

mistakeCategories.name: Sign error moving a term across the equals sign
mistakeCategories.description: Student moves a term but keeps its original sign.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Compute the answer that results from adding instead of subtracting (or vice-versa) the constant term.

mistakeCategories.name: Solved to coefficient·x but forgot the final division
mistakeCategories.description: Student isolates the variable term but reports its value before dividing by the coefficient.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: From 2x = 8, reports x = 8 instead of x = 4.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the value of the variable BEFORE dividing by its coefficient.

mistakeCategories.name: Distributed across only the first term in parentheses
mistakeCategories.description: Student multiplies the factor into the first term but not the rest.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Compute the result of distributing to only the first term inside the parentheses.

mistakeCategories.name: Solved for x when the question asks for an expression
mistakeCategories.description: Question asks for the value of e.g. 3x or x+1, but the student stops at x.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the value of x itself when the question asks for a multiple or expression of x.
